description:
Sub-task 3 follow-up to livespec li-qt7t5t. The upstream spec (livespec PR #184 v075) and the impl-plaintext sub-spec (PR #18 v003) declare that capture-impl-gaps's detection step invokes the sibling detect-impl-gaps thin-transport skill. Currently the capture-impl-gaps SKILL.md prose (Step 1) still describes scanning the spec via the Spec Reader directly, which duplicates detect-impl-gaps's logic in-skill and contradicts the contract ("no in-skill duplication of the detection logic; both this skill and doctor consume the same canonical surface").

This is a pure SKILL.md prose change. No Python edits required. Step 1 should be rewritten to invoke `detect-impl-gaps --json` via subprocess for the authoritative gap-id set, and (optionally) without `--json` for rich human-readable context to display alongside per-gap user consent. The opening prose and frontmatter description should be updated to reflect that `detect-impl-gaps` (not the Spec Reader directly) is the load-bearing surface this skill composes.

Acceptance:
- Step 1 of skills/capture-impl-gaps/SKILL.md describes subprocess invocation of detect-impl-gaps.
- The frontmatter description and opening prose no longer reference scanning via the Spec Reader directly.
- No Python changes (the existing Python primitives are unchanged).
- The lefthook + just check aggregates pass.
title: Refactor capture-impl-gaps SKILL.md Step 1 to invoke detect-impl-gaps --json (Sub-task 3 of livespec li-qt7t5t)

description: Close-out: capture-impl-gaps SKILL.md Step 1 refactored to invoke detect-impl-gaps.
reason: Closed. The capture-impl-gaps SKILL.md is rewritten to invoke the sibling detect-impl-gaps thin-transport skill instead of scanning the spec via Spec Reader directly. Changes: (1) frontmatter description updated to drop the Spec Reader phrasing; (2) opening prose updated so detect-impl-gaps + store are named as the load-bearing surfaces; (3) Step 1 rewritten to subprocess detect-impl-gaps --json for the authoritative gap-id set, plus a second invocation without --json for the rich line form, joined by gap_id for human display; (4) Step 3 updated to use the gap_id from Step 1 (was: derive locally) and correct the gap_id format documentation; (5) §What this skill does NOT do refined to note all spec reads are delegated. Pure SKILL.md prose change; no Python edits. Full just check aggregate (158 tests, 100% coverage) passes.
title: Refactor capture-impl-gaps SKILL.md Step 1 to invoke detect-impl-gaps --json (Sub-task 3 of livespec li-qt7t5t)

description:
Cross-repo follow-up from livespec's /livespec:revise v081 (PC #2: coordinating-epic-stale-revise-enforcement, cross-cutting epic). This is the child PC `work-item-merge-evidence` cited in the parent PC's §"Coordinating-epic header".

Scope (per the child PC at livespec-impl-plaintext/SPECIFICATION/proposed_changes/work-item-merge-evidence.md if it has been filed; otherwise FILE THAT PC FIRST via /livespec:propose-change against this repo's spec target):
- Extend the work-items JSONL schema with a `merge_evidence` field   that records the merged-to-master commit + branch + PR for every   closed work-item that traversed a feature branch.
- Implement a static check that fires `fail` on any closed work-item   whose merge_evidence is null/missing despite the existence of an   unmerged feature branch in git topology.
- Add a migration script that backfills merge_evidence for already-  closed work-items by walking git log for the work-item id.
- Update capture-work-item / implement skills to populate merge_evidence   at closure time.

Per the parent PC body's §"Cross-cutting acceptance criteria": the epic is complete when (item 2) this child PC is revised + accepted in livespec-impl-plaintext. Until then, the LayerDifferent surfacer (Layer 3' doctor LLM phase, filed in livespec's queue) is the residual guardrail.
title: Implement work-item-merge-evidence schema fields per sibling child PC

description:
Cross-repo follow-up from livespec's /livespec:revise v081 (PC #2: coordinating-epic-stale-revise-enforcement, Sub-proposal 3 / Layer 2). Sibling repo work to honor the new contract.

Scope:
- Copy /data/projects/livespec/.github/workflows/auto-enable-merge.yml   byte-identically into livespec-impl-plaintext's .github/workflows/.
- Verify the App is installed on the repo (GitHub App with   enablePullRequestAutoMerge permission scope).
- Set repo secrets APP_ID + APP_PRIVATE_KEY via `gh secret set`.
- Test by opening any non-draft PR with author=thewoolleyman; verify   it auto-enables --auto --rebase.

Per the contract: drift between this file and livespec's canonical version will surface as a doctor-static finding via dev-tooling's check_branch_protection_alignment (or sibling check).
resolution: superseded by li-mri7z6 — auto-enable-merge.yml propagated cross-repo via parent work-item; this child work-item is redundant
title: Add .github/workflows/auto-enable-merge.yml to livespec-impl-plaintext

description:
Follow-up impl work-item from livespec /livespec:revise v082 (livespec PC #3: revise-post-step-capture-impl-gaps, Layer 5 of the stale-revise-enforcement coordinating epic).

The livespec spec-side acceptance (v082) documents that the revise post-step invokes the active impl plugin's capture-impl-gaps skill with --since-version <prior-vN> to scope gap detection to the diff just introduced by the revise. The companion PC for this flag lives in this repo at SPECIFICATION/proposed_changes/capture-impl-gaps-since-version.md (filed 2026-05-26 — see commit 8d9d6b1 of this repo).

Scope:
- Implement --since-version <vNNN> flag on the capture-impl-gaps   thin-transport / heavyweight skill in   .claude-plugin/skills/capture-impl-gaps/SKILL.md and the   backing detect-impl-gaps thin-transport (per the spec-side   contract; capture-impl-gaps consults detect-impl-gaps).
- The flag scopes the gap-detection scan to the spec diff   between <prior-vN> and the latest cut version, instead of   re-detecting every long-standing gap.
- Without scoping, the livespec revise post-step would noisily   re-prompt for every legacy gap on every revise; with scoping,   only NEW gaps introduced by this revise surface — the   post-step's signal value stays high.
- Depends-on order: this work-item lands BEFORE livespec's   SKILL.md prose work-item (currently li-pzn5yp in livespec's   work-items.jsonl) can actually invoke the flag — the prose   references the flag prospectively; the IMPLEMENTATION needs   the flag to exist.
- Acceptance: the companion PC at   SPECIFICATION/proposed_changes/capture-impl-gaps-since-version.md   describes the full contract this work-item realizes.
title: Implement --since-version flag on capture-impl-gaps skill (Layer 5 of stale-revise enforcement)

description:
Follow-up impl work-item from livespec /livespec:revise v083 (livespec PC #4: spec-impl-commitment-tracking, sub-proposal 3 — extend-impl-plugin-contract-with-spec-commitment-hint).

The livespec spec-side acceptance (v083) declares that every impl-plugin's work-item record schema MUST carry an OPTIONAL spec_commitment_hint: string | null field, and that capture-work-item MUST accept an optional --spec-commitment-hint <id_hint> CLI flag (or backend-equivalent invocation surface).

Scope (in this repo):
- Add spec_commitment_hint: str | None field to WorkItem dataclass in .claude-plugin/scripts/livespec_impl_plaintext/types.py.
- Plumb the field through store.py's read/write paths (default null on read for legacy records; explicit serialization on write).
- Add --spec-commitment-hint <id_hint> flag to the capture-work-item skill surface (.claude-plugin/skills/capture-work-item/SKILL.md) and the backing Python entry point — when the flag is supplied, the resulting work-item's spec_commitment_hint MUST equal the verbatim id_hint; when omitted, the field defaults to null.
- Expose the field in list-work-items --json output (.claude-plugin/scripts/bin/list_work_items.py and its commands sibling) so livespec's unresolved-spec-commitment doctor invariant can match without invoking implementation-private machinery.
- Data migration: legacy work-items.jsonl records lacking the field MUST be readable with spec_commitment_hint defaulting to null (no in-place migration required; the field is OPTIONAL).
- Add --with-spec-commitment-hint <id_hint> filter flag to list-work-items for users who want to query by hint.
- Add unit + integration tests covering: write with flag, write without flag (null default), list-work-items --json output shape, filter flag behavior, legacy-record read compatibility.
- Reference: livespec SPECIFICATION/contracts.md §"Implementation-plugin contract — the 10-skill surface" → "Work-item `spec_commitment_hint` field" (landed in livespec v083 PC #4).

Depends-on order: this work-item lands BEFORE livespec's unresolved-spec-commitment doctor invariant module (currently li-7jniti in livespec's work-items.jsonl) can actually match against spec_commitment_hint records — the doctor invariant queries this skill's list-work-items --json output.
title: Add spec_commitment_hint field to work-item schema and --spec-commitment-hint flag to capture-work-item skill

description:
Cross-repo follow-up impl work-item from livespec /livespec:revise v085 (livespec PC #6: next-layer-3-discoverability-nudge).

The livespec spec change (landed in PR #262) adds a contract clause to SPECIFICATION/contracts.md §"Implementation-plugin contract — the 10-skill surface" → bullet `next` requiring every impl-plugin's `next` SKILL.md to surface a Layer 3 discoverability nudge on direct user invocation, parallel-and-symmetric to the /livespec:next requirement.

Scope:
- Edit .claude-plugin/skills/next/SKILL.md in this repo to add the discoverability nudge prose.
- The nudge MUST inform the user that .claude/skills/loop/SKILL.md (the project-local Layer 3 loop driver) is the cohesive cross-side composition surface combining /livespec:next with the active impl-plugin's next (i.e., this repo's `next` skill), and MUST ask the user to confirm direct invocation rather than via /loop.
- The nudge MUST skip when this skill is invoked by another skill (Layer 3 driver itself, doctor cross-boundary surface, etc.) rather than by a direct user request. Detection mechanism is per-harness.
- The impl-plugin author MAY specialize the wording to reference the JSONL substrate (this repo's backend) but MUST preserve the load-bearing semantics: inform the user that the Layer 3 driver is the cohesive cross-side surface, and ask them to confirm direct invocation.
- The wrapper at .claude-plugin/scripts/bin/next.py MUST remain a pure thin-transport pass-through; the nudge is SKILL.md-prose discipline only.
- For projects where .claude/skills/loop/SKILL.md is absent, the nudge MAY soften to a documentation pointer.
- Reference: livespec SPECIFICATION/contracts.md §"Implementation-plugin contract — the 10-skill surface" → bullet `next` (final clause), and §"`/livespec:next` spec-side thin-transport skill" → §"Layer 3 discoverability nudge" for the load-bearing semantics it references.

Paired same-repo follow-up in livespec is li-x6zfw8 (livespec PR #263) for that repo's own /livespec:next SKILL.md.
title: Add Layer 3 discoverability nudge prose to skills/next/SKILL.md (per livespec PC #6)
